title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland canceled. And, DHS to give police facial recognition app
description: The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.

title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

title: These Wisconsin swing voters say Trump's war in Iran wasn't worth it
description: The war in Iran was a costly blunder, according to Wisconsin swing voters who participated in two online focus groups that NPR observed.

title: Juneteenth: How news of the Emancipation Proclamation spread through the South
description: While some enslaved people did not know about Lincoln's order, many learned of it while the fighting was still ongoing through informal networks, rumors and sometimes from slaveholders themselves.

title: It's toys vs. tech in 'Toy Story 5.' Here are 4 ways to keep tech in check this summer
description: Kids' screen use goes way up in the summertime. And just as the movie Toy Story 5 portrays, that can be problematic for children. Here are tips for parents to help their kids manage screens and have fun IRL this summer.

title: Some local police have access to an ICE facial recognition app
description: A document from the Department of Homeland Security outlines plans to issue local police facial recognition technology used by federal immigration agents, a move that will expand the scope of ICE surveillance.

title: How the 1874 Freedman's Bank collapse connects to economic disparities we see today
description: In Savings and Trust, historian Justene Hill Edwards tells the story of the Freedman's Bank, which was created for formerly enslaved people following the Civil War. Originally broadcast Nov. 7, 2024.

title: Why did the newly refurbished Reflecting Pool turn green? The quiz knows
description: Plus, keep an eye out for our World Cup pun, intrigue around a tarp, and the Obama Presidential Center.
